title: lupinus latifolius var. subalpinus
intro: Lupinus latifolius var. subalpinus is a variety of the subalpine lupine, a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It is a perennial herb with a rich history of use in traditional medicine, valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat a range of health issues. The plant's health benefits include reducing swelling and pain, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Lupinus latifolius var. subalpinus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
